user_query:
I'm a long-time Hillsong UNITED fan in Australia working on a little setlist tool that exports JSON. To sanity-check our JSON handling, I wrote a tiny downloader to pull the official JSON specification (RFC 8259) as a test input, but it’s buggy and only writes a truncated HTML page instead of the text spec. Please debug and fix the script at input/downloader.py so that it reliably downloads the official plain-text RFC 8259 from the RFC Editor website (domain: rfc-editor.org) using the RFC number from input/config.json, saves the full, unmodified text to the configured path, and writes consistent metadata. Use the existing config at input/config.json and do not hardcode absolute paths or the URL; derive the correct text resource from the RFC number and the official source.

Requirements:
- Inputs: input/downloader.py (buggy), input/config.json (contains rfc_number: 8259 and output paths).
- Outputs (must be created by a successful run):
  1) workspace/output/rfc-8259.txt — the complete plain-text of RFC 8259 downloaded from the official RFC Editor site.
  2) workspace/output/metadata.json — a JSON object with these keys: rfc_number (int), source_domain (string set to "rfc-editor.org"), bytes_downloaded (int), sha256_hex (lowercase hex), first_line_text (string), downloaded_at_iso8601 (timestamp string). The sha256_hex must be the checksum of the saved file’s exact bytes. bytes_downloaded must equal the saved file’s byte size. first_line_text must exactly match the first line in workspace/output/rfc-8259.txt.
  3) workspace/report/fix-report.md — a concise report containing: Summary (what was broken), Root cause, Fix (what you changed and why), Verification (include the final sha256 and byte count and how you checked internal consistency), and Next steps.

Notes and constraints:
- The script should fetch the official plain-text version of RFC 8259 from the RFC Editor (rfc-editor.org). Avoid the HTML "info" page; download the actual text RFC. Handle redirects and ensure the entire file is read, not just a partial buffer.
- Respect the configured output paths from input/config.json: workspace/output/rfc-8259.txt and workspace/output/metadata.json. Create any missing directories if needed.
- Save the downloaded RFC bytes without altering them; compute the SHA-256 digest over those exact bytes.
- Write metadata.json as structured JSON exactly once the file is saved, ensuring internal consistency among fields as stated above.
- Please do not remove the separate report deliverable. Place your status summary at workspace/report/fix-report.md.

When you're done, I should be able to run the fixed script locally, see the full RFC text saved at workspace/output/rfc-8259.txt, read the structured metadata in workspace/output/metadata.json, and review your fix and verification notes in workspace/report/fix-report.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
